lessons.title: Cell Structure and Function
objectives: Identify parts of animal and plant cells
Explain the function of cell organelles
concepts: Differences between plant and animal cells (cell wall, vacuoles).

experiment.description: Cell Lab: Drag labels (Nucleus, Mitochondria, etc.) to the correct locations on a diagram of a plant and animal cell.

lessons.title: Diffusion and Osmosis
objectives: Predict the movement of molecules in various concentrations
concepts: Diffusion: Movement from high to low concentration.
Osmosis: Movement of water across a semi-permeable membrane.

experiment.description: Osmosis Sim: Place a 'cell' in solutions of different salt concentrations and watch it shrink or swell in real-time.

lessons.title: Photosynthesis
objectives: Identify the factors affecting the rate of photosynthesis
concepts: Factors: Light intensity, CO2 concentration, temperature.

experiment.description: Photosynthesis Lab: Adjust sliders for light intensity and temperature to see how the rate of oxygen bubble production changes in an aquatic plant.

lessons.title: The Circulatory System
objectives: Identify parts of the human heart
Trace the path of blood through the body
concepts: Arteries carry blood away from the heart; Veins carry blood towards the heart.

experiment.description: Blood Flow Tracker: Click on parts of the heart in the correct order to successfully pump blood from the right atrium to the aorta.

lessons.title: Aerobic and Anaerobic Respiration
objectives: Explain the production of lactic acid during exercise
concepts: Aerobic: uses oxygen, produces more energy.
Anaerobic: no oxygen, produces lactic acid (in humans).

experiment.description: Respiration Race: Choose between 'Oxygen' and 'No Oxygen' modes for a runner and see how quickly energy (ATP) depletes and lactic acid builds up.

lessons.title: Food Chains and Webs
objectives: Construct food chains and food webs
concepts: Energy flow in an ecosystem starts with the sun.

experiment.description: Ecosystem Builder: Drag and drop organisms (grass, grasshopper, frog, hawk) to create a functional food web. If one population is removed, watch the impact on others.

lessons.title: DNA and Inheritance
objectives: Explain the role of chromosomes and genes
Use Punnett squares to predict inheritance

experiment.description: Breed-a-Pet: Select genotypes for two parent pets (e.g., Bb for brown fur) and use a Punnett square to predict the colors of their offspring.
